elements.instruction: The requirement is 100% finished and 80% packed in the box.
elements.expectedResult:
New Era prefers production orders to be 100% sewing finished and 80% packed into the box for pre-shipment inspection (PSI). 

Once the factory can’t reach the quantity requirement, the Inspector informs the protocol team, and the protocol team emails the client the product status

If the product is not 100% finished and packed but 60-80% finished. Client accepts DUPRO Inspections (during production) and inspector remarks into the report “This is dupro inspection because goods are xx% finished”

If less than 60% are finished, the Quality team sends an email to the client and the client decides if the inspection should go forward at the lower completion level or missing If no timely feedback before noon time 12:00. please continue the inspection and fail the quantity check.

Please input the total quantity finished and packed in pcs and %, the total quantity finished and unpacked in pcs and %.
Pls follow the below template on your comment.
XXXXPcs(XX%) Finished&Packed
XXXXPcs(XX%) Finished&Unpacked
elements.name: Available Quantity Check

elements.instruction:
Were you able to select the cartons / sampling size fully randomly ? 
If yes, the result is passed, If not, the result is failed.
elements.expectedResult:
1. Take a picture of the stacks of cartons before selection​
2. Choose cartons from every pallet/stack.​
3. Choose cartons from different parts of the stack (top, bottom, left, right, front, back, middle, inside), including not accessible cartons ​
4. Ask factory for support in moving the goods if they are not accessible​
5. Take a picture of the stack after the selecting the cartons, showing how the random selection was done. ​
6. Take a picture of all selected cartons​
7. Follow the cartons from the warehouse to the inspection area, make sure they are not tempered with.  ​
8. Add a remark in the report and inform back-office if you cannot proceed with the random carton selection, explaining the reason.
elements.name: Random carton selection

elements.expectedResult:
Upload a picture of the stacks / pallets before the carton selection following the example of attached picture.

Limit the "Carton Select" photos to:
- one (1) Carton Before Selection
elements.name: Cartons before selection

elements.expectedResult:
Upload a picture of the stacks / pallets after the carton selection following the example of attached picture.
Through this picture, the client / back-office should be able to verify that the carton selection has been done randomly.
elements.name: Cartons after selection

elements.expectedResult:
Upload a picture of all selected cartons following the example of attached picture.

Limit the "Carton Select" photos to:
- one (1) Selected Carton
elements.name: Selected cartons

elements.expectedResult:
Limit the "Carton Select" photos to:
- two (2) The environment of the Warehouse & Carton Storage

actions.instructions:
For Headwear/Cap inspection, any out-of-tolerance found for the “Circumference” must be added to the AQL table as a critical defect. 

Example: If you find 5 caps out of 60 caps with head circumference out of tolerance, please add 5 CRITICAL defects in the defect table.

Note: In the meantime, the measurement result should remain at “N/A” with a comment “Subject to Client’s Evaluation”.

testsChecklist.name: New Era Cap- Cap - Product Dimensions

elements.instruction:
1/ No need to add screencap the measurement Excel
2/ No need to screen cut the Excel, no need for measurement picture of each POM
3/ Any out-of-tolerance found for the “Circumference” must be added to the AQL table as a critical defect. 
4/ Please always include the picture of CAP measurement (size, circumference, visor height and shape) on randomly selected POM
elements.expectedResult:
1.The cap sample size is 60: 20pcs for each silhouette measurement on the circumference, and shape, visor height, back opening height. 6pcs of each with Seam 1, Seam 2, Seam 3, Left to Right Middle Panel measurement. if the measurement is beyond the tolerance and it should be called a Major defect, it should be reflected and counted into the workmanship appearance column.So the AQL is 2.5.
2.If the number of style > 10, pls follow sample size in the booking.
elements.name: Product Dimensions Result

columns.name: Total # of POM out of tolerance
Total # of POM out of tolerance

actions.instructions:
1. Report writing - The Inspector must add a remark to indicate any re-inspection. 
- Example of Inspector's remark format: This is re-inspection from R-Cloud-25083616

2. Special Checkpoint - The inspector must pay attention to some special checkpoints for the "NFL" PO as per the attached file. 

3. Specification check for CAP:
- If 10 or less than 10 logos - check all logos
- If 20 or more than 10 logos - check 10 logos and randomly take pictures of 3 - 5 logos (according to the picture requirement).

4. For garments, follow the client specs tolerance.

metadata.filename: Special Checkpoints for  NFL sideline orders (Cap).pdf

elements.instruction:
1. Use a calibrated scale to measure the actual weight of the product against specifications or reference sample. 
2. If not specified, the tolerance is ?? 5%.
elements.expectedResult: Is the weight to specifications or reference sample within tolerances?
elements.name: Weight Check

elements.instruction:
1. Cut the material to standardized size and number and from specified sections for use with the GSM tester. 
2. Compare to the specifications.
elements.expectedResult:
Is the weight of the material to specifications?
The inspector must comment whether it is "within tolerance or out of tolerance"
elements.name: Fabric Weight Test

elements.instruction:
1. Smell the product, checking for any odor.
2. Compare to that of the reference sample.
elements.expectedResult: Does the product meet requirment?
elements.name: Odor Check

elements.instruction: Rub the product or label with a white cotton cloth soaked with water ten times over a 10 cm distance with a force of 9N (1 kg).
elements.expectedResult:
For general colors: Wet rubbing result shall be 3.0 or above on the gray scale.
For dark shades / indigo / pigment dye / garment dyed / flannel / pile fabric / denim fabric: Wet rubbing result shall be 2–3 or above on the gray scale.

This test does not apply to white, cream, or ivory colors.

comment.message:
Checked no Issues
But on MI# 60978487 and 60978504 already indicated in the caps color transfer

elements.name: Wet Test

elements.instruction: Rub the product or label with a dry white cotton cloth ten times over a 10 cm distance with a force of 9 N (1 kg).
elements.expectedResult:
For general colors: Dry rubbing result shall be 4.0 or above on the gray scale.
For dark shades / indigo / pigment dye / garment dyed / flannel / pile fabric / denim fabric: Dry rubbing result shall be 3–4 or above on the gray scale.

This test does not apply to white, cream, or ivory colors.

comment.message: Photo was attached Note that MI #60978504 and 60978487 has already a notes label indicated that color transfer will occur

elements.name: Dry Test

elements.instruction:
Mandatory to perform cap moisture check on 4 points (front panel, visor (upper and lower), sweatband).
- The MOISTURE TEST DATA needs to be submitted along with the New Era Cap LLC_Measurement Defect AQL Table.

1. Use machine provided by the factory, measure the product.
2. Check that the cartons are on pallets (no contact with walls / floor, at least 0.5m away from window), dry (check the bottom of cartons) and without moisture or mold. 
3. Check that the storing areas for cartons, products, fabrics and materials are clean, dry, with ventilation and without mold / water on walls. 
4. Check that the storing area's humidity is to / below specifications.
elements.expectedResult:
1. The number on the machine should be meeting New Era Cap tolerance (atttached)
2. Are the cartons stored in correct conditions and free from any effect from moisture?
3. Are the storage areas clean, dry, ventilated and without presence of molde/ water ? 
4. Is the humidity follow specifications?
elements.name: Moisture Test for Cap

elements.instruction: Pull button, snap, hook, bar, handle or attachment with the specified force and duration.
elements.expectedResult: Does any part of the product break, get deformed or damaged in anyway during or after use?
elements.name: Pull Test

elements.instruction:
1. Ensure the machine isolate the packing area from the production line so that 100% of the pieces in the packing line went through the machine.
2. Check the calibration of the metal detection machine.
3. Scan the whole sampling size with the machine.
elements.expectedResult:
1. No foreign metal objects are found on product?
2. Is the calibration available and up to date?
elements.name: Metal Detection Check

elements.instruction: Pull the product by hand at the seam location to test the strength of the seam.
elements.expectedResult: Does the seam break?
elements.name: Seam Strength test

elements.instruction:
1. Use the product on a model, doll or dummy and verify that there is no issues. 
2. Test with each size available.
elements.expectedResult: Is the product correctly sized and balanced?
elements.name: Model Fitting Check

elements.instruction: Count the number of stitches per inch compared to specifications or reference sample.
elements.expectedResult: Are the number of stitches per inch correct to specifications or reference sample?
elements.name: Stitch Density Check

elements.instruction: Check the stuffing of the item is stuffed.
elements.expectedResult: Is the product clean, dry and free from any foreign object?
elements.name: Stuffing Check

elements.instruction: 1. Check that the colors are conform to the specifications, and record the corresponding pantone code and deviation from the expected result.
elements.expectedResult: Is the product in the correct shade and color inside of the allowable 4-5 color shading range?
elements.name: Color Specification Check

elements.instruction:
1. Put the inspected pieces next to each other.
2. Identify any deviation in color from one sample to the other.
3. Check the color shading against the reference sample.
elements.expectedResult: 1. Is the deviation within the acceptable range as per the specifications? (If there is no specification available, use grey scale 4 as the acceptable limit.)
elements.name: Color Shading Check

elements.instruction:
1. Check that there is no obvious damage from needles from construction.
2. Pull the seams by hand using force (70N for 10s) to inspect the needle holes for defects.
elements.expectedResult: There shall be no needle damage.
elements.name: Needle damage check

elements.instruction:
1. Connect scanner to a PC and scan barcode. 
2. Scan any barcode on the product and compare the results to the code under the barcodes (if any) and to the specifications.
elements.expectedResult:
1. Does the barcode match the specifications? 
2. Is the barcode scannable and have the correct number?
elements.name: Barcode Scanning Test

elements.instruction: Check the dimensions, shape, style or color of the product against the approval sample.
elements.expectedResult: The products should be matched with the approval sample.
elements.name: Approval sample comparison

actions.instructions:
Follow strictly defects from DCL aligned with client.
If a defect is missing, select the closest one in the list and add details in defect caption. Escalate also to ITL/SIC to add defect in DCL

defects.name: Exposed Embroidery Foam
defects.description: Exposed Embroidery Foam

defects.name: Color Shading
defects.description:
Standard Requirement:
A Color Shade differences between the panels is NOT allowed.
Color Shade difference between the panels and the visor is NOT allowed.
A Color Shade difference between the crown of the cap and the button is NOT allowed.
Exception: occasionally the fusing process may cause a slight discoloration of the Front Panels in certain fabrics; this
is acceptable ONLY if the discoloration could be considered not noticeable to the average customer.

defects.name: Fabric Flaws
defects.description:
Non-Acceptable Flaws:

Any obvious fabric flaws or runs inside or outside of caps are NOT acceptable.
Fabric flaws or runs in the Front Panels are NOT acceptable.
Any flaws near logo or eyelet area are NOT acceptable (gives appearance of run in the fabric).
Fabric Flaws or Runs on the upper Visor are NOT acceptable.
Holes or Runs are NOT acceptable anywhere on the cap.
Worn Spots are NOT acceptable anywhere on the cap.
Missing Weave or Stitches are NOT acceptable.
Raised bumps or knots in the fabric are NOT acceptable.
Fabric flaws of a different color than the cap fabric are NOT acceptable (dark or light).

Acceptable Flaws:

Fabric flaws that are flat and of the same color and are on the undervisor, back, or middle panels.
Fabric flaws of a slightly different color and can only be noticed at a very close glance.

defects.name: Printing or Sublimation Defects
defects.description:
Obviously Miss-aligned or poorly registered printing / sublimation is NOT acceptable.
Smudging is NOT acceptable.
Missing printing / sublimation is NOT acceptable.
Shaded / color fade is NOT acceptable.
Ink stains / spots are NOT acceptable.
Flaws in the printed surface is NOT acceptable (bubbles, drips, foreign particles) .

defects.name: Unopened Seam Defects
defects.description:
Fold / Bump Inside Seam - Slight / 1
Instance

defects.name: Unopened Seam Defects
defects.description:

Fold / Bump Inside Seam – Severe 3+ instances or visible through shear
Fold / Bump Inside Seam – obvious
Fabric Twisted / Folded to one side within Seam
Fabric Folded to One Side within

defects.description:
1. puckering over length of seam.
2. Severe puckering.

defects.description: Smooth with slight puckering

defects.name: Puckering: Non-Stretch Fabrics
defects.description:
1. puckering over length of seam.
2. severe puckering.

defects.name: Uneven/Crooked Seams
defects.description:
1. +/- > 1.6MM Line to Seam.
2. curving
3. twisted seam
4. irregular seam

defects.name: Broken Stitches
defects.description: Inner stitches show w/ slight pulling of fabric

defects.name: Broken Stitches
defects.description:
1. stitches show w/ tearing of the fabric.
2. inner taping shows through the seam.
3. broken stitches.

defects.name: Damaged Buttons
defects.description:
General Rule: damaged buttons are always considered a Major defect.
Note: slight fabric discoloration on some materials will occur due to pressures required to make and
attach buttons. This is not considered as a defect.

defects.name: Press Marks
defects.description:
Note: slight fabric discoloration on some materials will occur due to pressures required to make and attach buttons. This is not considered as a defect.

Note that in cases where extreme press marks are found and are determined to the result of incorrect machine settings NE reserves the to class as a defect.

defects.name: Eyelet Placement
defects.description:
Standard Requirement - Front Panels: 
A noticeable vertical (up/down) asymmetry in eyelets >2MM is classified as a major defect.
A noticeable horizontal (left/right) asymmetry in eyelets >2MM is classified as a major defect.
Standard Requirement – Back/Middle
Panels: a noticeable vertical (up/down) asymmetry in eyelets >5MM is classified as a major defect.
A noticeable horizontal (left/right) asymmetry in eyelets  >5MM is classified as a major defect.

defects.name: Eyelet Untrimmed Threads
defects.description:
Standard Requirement - Exterior:
Any untrimmed thread >0.1CM is classified as a major defect.
Standard Requirement - Interior:
Any untrimmed thread >0.5CM is classified as a major defect.

defects.name: Eyelet Unopened
defects.description: Any unopened eyelet is classified as a major defect.

defects.name: Lopsided Eyelet
defects.description: Any lopsided eyelet is classified as a major defect.

defects.name: Lopsided Eyelet
defects.description: Side and back panel eyelets that have been stretched out of shape during the blocking process and can easily be corrected by hand are to be considered to be a non-critical defect.

defects.name: Non-Circular: Eyelet Not Centered on Pre-Punched Eyelet Hole
defects.description: Any noncircular eyelet is classified as a major defect.

defects.name: Torn Fabric
defects.description: Any torn or damaged fabric around the eyelet is considered a major defect.

defects.name: Missing Eyelet
defects.description:
Missing eyelets are always considered a critical defect unless specifically
called out for on the spec sheet.

defects.name: Poorly Sewn Eyelet
defects.description: Poorly Sewn eyelets are considered a major defect.

defects.name: Eyelet Oversized - Inner Circumference.
defects.description: Overly large inner eyelets are considered a major defect.

defects.name: Sharp Points
defects.description: Sharp Points are always considered a major defect.

defects.name: Missing Stitching
defects.description: missing stitching on eyelets is a major defect.

defects.name: Visor Alignment Defects
defects.description:
Not Acceptable: 
Visor is noticeably crooked and left and right front-seams are misaligned >2MM on the visor as referenced by the left and right peak-stitching.

defects.name: Visor Point Defects
defects.description: Not Acceptable: Visor points are obviously asymmetrical > 2MM

defects.name: Undervisor Visible
defects.description: Excessive undervisor is noticeably visible from top of the visor >0.1CM

defects.name: Undervisor Visible
defects.description: slight amount of undervisor is noticeably visible from top of the visor <0.1CM

defects.name: Puckering: Curved Undervisor
defects.description: Heavy Puckering

Dirty or Stained Outer Panels

1 Small, non-obvious stains /dirt inside the caps <0.2CM is considered a Non-critical Defect.
2 or more Small, non-obvious stains /dirt inside the caps <0.2CM is considered a Non-critical Defect.

defects.description:
General Rule: damaged buttons are always considered a Major defect.
Note: slight fabric discoloration on some materials will occur due to pressures required to make and attach buttons. This is not considered as a defect.

defects.name: Damaged Fabric
defects.description: Any torn or damaged fabric around the eyelet is considered a major defect.

Missing eyelets are always considered a critical defect unless specifically

defects.name: Sweatband Low at the Visor Bottom Edge
Label Extends Beyond the Finished Edge or Sweatband
Exposed Embroidery EVA
defects.description: Exposed Embroidery EVA

defects.name: Seam 2 & 3 Front Shape
defects.description:
Requirement: Front side seams are straight with no curving, puckering or waving. 
Defect*: Front side seams are curved inward at the temples (hipping), wavy at temples or puckered at the seams.
*All the below examples will be classified as a major defect
Root Cause: Incomplete blocking, incorrect blocking head size, failure to hand shape after removing from blocking head and/or improper handling and packing.

defects.name: Seam 1 Front Shape
defects.description:
Requirement: Seam 1 should lay flat with no peaking and no caving in at top of crown.
Defect*: Center front seam is higher than the sides causing a peak at top of crown. Top of front crown panels curve or depress downward.
*All the below examples will be classified as a major defect
Root Cause: Blocker does not effectively use sandbag to shape top of cap and/or cap not initially placed correctly on blocking head.

defects.name: Flat Visor Shape
defects.description:
Requirement: Visor should lay flush against a flat surface with no bending or curving. 
Defect*: Visor does not lay flush against a flat surface with a curve or bend of 1mm or more. Entire visor is off balance. 
*All the below examples will be classified as a major defect
Root Cause: Visors are not handled properly in production line, incorrect peak attach top stitching, incorrect sewing gesture during assembly and curve is not corrected in blocking line.

defects.name: Curved Visor Shape- 4.5cm
defects.description:
Requirement: Visor should be curved symmetrically and meet the 4.5cm height standard. 
Defect*: Visor does not meet the required height. Visor curve is off center. 
*All the below examples will be classified as a major defect
Root Cause: Visors are not properly placed into the visor curve machine, peak stitching is not done on curved visor machine, not properly cooled on racks.

defects.name: Curved Visor- 2.5cm
defects.description:
Requirement: Visor should be curved symmetrically and meet the 2.5cm height standard. 
Defect*: Visor does not meet the required height. Visor curve is off center. 
*All the below examples will be classified as a major defect
Root Cause: Visors are not properly placed into the visor curve machine, not properly cooled on racks.

defects.name: Front Fusing
defects.description:
Requirement: Buckram must be fully adhered to front cap and lay smooth with no bubbling, puckering or waving. 
Defect*: Buckram is not fully adhered, fit front process is not done properly, and front panels are not smooth. There is visible waving, denting and puckering. 
*All the below examples will be classified as a major defect
Root Cause: Fit fronts are not properly fused. Incorrect size of buckram, excessive stretching during fit front process, failure to follow sewing sequence for fit front. Time and temp not properly set up, machine not equipped with top and bottom heat, machine not maintenance. Workers not properly trained and no QC.

defects.name: Incorrect Crown Shape for Silhouette
defects.description:
Requirement: Crown shape must match required shape, fit the correct blocking head and profile gauge. 
Refer to global spec for each MID to determine blocking head, profile gauge and correct shape. 
Defect*: Crown shape does not match requirement or approved silhouette sample. Examples include: too rounded, too high or too square. Does not match profile gauge.
*All the below examples will be classified as a major defect
Root Cause: Incorrect blocking head (silhouette and size) was used, incorrect blocking method was used, QC did not check against global spec or profile gauge.

defects.name: Seams 1,2 & 3 Back Shape
defects.description:
Requirement: Entire crown shape must a smooth and consistent shape all around. Not folding or crushing.
Defect*: Back crown shape is not smooth due to folding or crushing, incorrect packing method.
*All the below examples will be classified as a major defect
Root Cause: Incorrect handling and packing after blocking

defects.name: Skipped Stitches
defects.description: Skipped Stitches

defects.name: Poor assembly / construction
defects.description:
1. The product or parts (assembly) is not fitting well together or has to be forced together.
2. There are gaps in between parts where there shouldn't be.

defects.name: Wrinkles / Puckering / Creases / Waves
defects.description: The product contains wrinkles or creases visible from 1m away.

defects.name: Untrimmed Thread Ends
defects.description:
Untrimmed thread ends, raw edge of surface (edge, buttonhole opening) or knots found at the surface.
If the product is:
- for children: defect is larger than 0.5 cm, 
- others: defect is larger than:
                     * 2.5 cm outside
                     * 10 cm inside

3. Did you mention the item number, PO, in the excel AQL? as per guideline, even 0 defect need to be mentioned.
4. Did you refer to the mandatory picture example in GI?

actions.text:
Issued by QIMA Inspection (Shenzhen) Limited
Reviewed by Joseph Wang

THE ABOVE RESULT(S) REFLECT(S) QIMA LIMITED’S FINDINGS AT THE TIME AND PLACE OF INSPECTION. THIS REPORT DOES NOT RELEASE THE BUYER OR SELLER FROM CONTRACTUAL RESPONSIBILITIES, NOR DOES IT PREJUDICE THE BUYER’S RIGHT OF CLAIM TOWARD THE SELLER/SUPPLIER FOR COMPENSATION FOR ANY APPARENT AND/OR HIDDEN DEFECTS NOT DETECTED DURING INSPECTION OR OCCURRING ANYTIME THEREAFTER.

globalRemark.message:
1-The products have been finished and packed. The inspected lot consisted of 71,059 pieces (1006 cartons), representing 80% of the planned shipment quantity of 88,824 pieces.
2-A total of 32 cartons were randomly selected for the purchase order (PO).
3-Inspected Carton Numbers:23,7,20,32,20,6,1,264,68,91,239,345,21,24,29,36,157,139,154,43,45,47,6,251,149,144,16,17,1,4,23,17
4-A random sample of 750 pieces was selected from the 32 cartons to cover the PO and items.
5-All defects found were presented to Ms. Chutaporn and agreed upon by, the factory
  representative.
6-Measurement specifications were provided by the factory for inspection.
7-Notes:
MI# 60930397 and 60930398 Seen puckering/wrinkles but the same as the Approval sample.
MI# 60978511 Seen some front embroidery not centered +/- 1,2MM
